content:
Goal
* Please add "in increasing index order" after "3 piles of dirt".

* Please format "h1", "h2" and "h3" as [[variables]] instead of {{constants}}.

Input
* Please move the guarantee to the Constraints section.

Thanks.
It's done, thanks for the feedback

Just to add on to @5DN1L's comments:

- For clarity, you may want to add that "the middle pile is strictly higher than the other two" means that [[h2]] > [[h1]] and [[h2]] > [[h3]].

- [[N]] does not seem to exceed 2500 in all test cases; please update the constraints to better reflect this.

- I would suggest including more test cases that cover larger values of N and/or larger pile height values. These larger values are currently only covered by the last two test cases.

Your first 2 points are done.
But I struggled to have a higher N in test cases. I wanted to have like 100 000 values but the text area seems to truncate the input data, so I wasn't able to have more than ~2500 values < 1000 and ~1000 values < 100 000. If you know it is possible to add more data, I certainly could do it
I can add more tests that cover thee constraints though.

That's fine; what I meant was to include more tests to cover a wider range of values of N. Currently, 6 tests have small (N < 20) input sizes, then the next test has N=50, followed by a jump to N=500, and then to N=2500. It would be helpful to include a more gradual progression of the input sizes to aid in puzzle solving.

If the text area does not allow for too many numbers, I suppose the only way would be to reduce the number of digits each individual dirt pile height can have (e.g. for large N, each dirt pile height can be <100). I would say this is a reasonable tradeoff since at larger Ns you're mainly just testing for the optimal algorithm runtime and so the dirt pile heights do not matter nearly as much.

A few other test cases I can think of:

- Please add some test cases with small N, but with a large variation in dirt pile heights.

- Please add some test cases where there is a large number of equal dirt pile heights.

Thank you!

Thanks for all your suggestions!
I've added these test cases:
- Fluctuations & Big heights (low N, high heights)
- So high! & Bigger & So many piles (increasing N, decreasing maximum height)
- Similar heights (N=3000, 10 different values of heights)
I also updated the constraints section as I increased maximum N
